footer.tagline: Turning complex capex projects into clear, quotable work across borders.

hero.subtitle: NovaNexus turns messy project threads into a shared, quotable map that buyers and vetted factories can actually work from.

pipeline.title: RFQ pipeline snapshot
pipeline.step1Body: Drawings, throughput and standards captured in a single clean brief.
pipeline.step2Body: Short-list of factories filtered by process, region and commercial envelope.
pipeline.step3Body: Comparable quotes, duty impact and risk calls surfaced in one view.
pipeline.cycleValue: 5–12 business days from RFQ to award

audience.title: A sourcing cockpit for both sides of the table.
audience.buyersBody: Plant managers, project engineers and owners see the same live version of the project instead of scattered files and endless email chains.
audience.suppliersBody: Factories and system integrators receive focused RFQs that match their real capabilities instead of generic blasts and tire-kickers.

hero.body: NovaNexus sits as a neutral layer between buyers and suppliers in precision manufacturing and industrial automation. The goal is simple:
hero.bodyStrong: fewer surprises before a PO is ever signed.

map.bodyPrefix: Processes, capacity and risk translated into
map.bodyStrong: a shared map both sides can work from.

pillars.heading: What actually lives behind the listings.
pillars.subheading: Most industrial directories maximise raw listing count. NovaNexus is deliberately narrow – centred on capability depth, realistic capacity and the actual effort to run a line, not just contact info.

capability.label: Capability map
capability.title: Processes, not just company logos.
capability.body: Every supplier is anchored on processes, materials, tolerances and certifications – so buyers can search the way engineers actually think.
bullets: Process tags across machining, extrusion, automation, clean-room and more.
Material coverage and typical tolerances surfaced up-front.
Certifications and industry focus at a glance.

rfqs.label: Structured RFQs
rfqs.title: RFQs that quote cleanly the first time.
rfqs.body: Guided RFQ intake removes guesswork so suppliers respond with real numbers instead of “call us to discuss”.
bullets: Required technical fields for drawings, volumes and quality expectations.
File upload for drawings, BOMs and reference photos.
Automatic hints when a critical detail is missing.

decision.label: Decision layer
decision.title: Playbooks layered on top of listings.
decision.body: Lightweight guides live right next to suppliers, so teams make faster, less political decisions.
bullets: Make-vs-buy and route comparison notes for common cases.
Checklists for first-time sourcing and new geographies.
Signals on where capacity and lead-times are shifting.

buyers.title: If you’re a buyer
buyers.copy: Built for operations, engineering and sourcing teams who need a shared, realistic map before they ever issue a PO.
benefits: A shortlist of realistic options by process, region and envelope.
Cleaner RFQs that get real quotes back, faster.
Early warnings on risk, lead time and rough cost bands.

suppliers.title: If you’re a supplier
suppliers.copy: Listing here is meant to feel lighter than a full corporate site, but far more meaningful than dropping a logo into a directory.
benefits: A way to show depth without hiring a marketing agency.
Context on what global buyers are actually asking for this year.
Signals on where to invest capacity and automation next.

roadmap.heading: Where this is going.
roadmap.copy: The roadmap is shaped around how real capex teams actually buy and build lines – not a shiny feature checklist. The more projects flow through NovaNexus, the sharper the guidance and benchmarking become.

now.title: Curated bench & structured intake
now.body: Curated supplier bench, structured RFQ intake and starter playbooks so both sides know what “good” looks like from day one.

next.title: Deeper filters, clearer bands
next.body: Deeper discovery filters, broader automation coverage and clearer cost / lead-time bands for common project types.

later.title: Analytics, heat-maps and field guides
later.body: Lightweight analytics for suppliers, region heat-maps for buyers and richer “how to scope it” field guides built from live project data.

cta.heading: Explore the ecosystem.
cta.copy: Browse suppliers, read through the guides and send a trial RFQ. Even if you’re just mapping options, you should leave with a clearer, more realistic picture of what’s possible for your next line or automation project.
cta.overline: Start from whichever side fits you.
cta.note: No fees or commitments to look around.

suppliers.heroTitle: A clean pipeline of realistic RFQs instead of noise.
suppliers.heroSubtitle: NovaNexus filters out weak leads so factories and SIs only see projects where they can realistically win and deliver.

benefits.fitBody: RFQs are matched on process window, envelope, certifications and install reach.
benefits.contextBody: You see throughput, duty assumptions, and risk flags up front instead of guessing through email.
benefits.supportBody: Support on scoping, quoting and install planning so you can focus on execution.

suppliers.ctaApply: Apply to become a vetted supplier

auth.notesLabel: What kind of projects are you planning or supplying?
auth.successMessage: Thanks — we’ve received your details. We’ll follow up shortly.
